item.title: Nonlocal conductance of a Majorana wire near the topological transition
item.description:
Author(s): Vladislav D. Kurilovich, William S. Cole, Roman M. Lutchyn, and Leonid I. Glazman

We develop a theory of the nonlocal conductance ${G}_{RL}(V)$ for a disordered Majorana wire tuned near the topological transition critical point. Under these conditions, the antisymmetric part of the differential conductance, $[{G}_{RL}(V)−{G}_{RL}(−V)]/2$, is the dominant one for a sufficiently lo…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 094526] Published Fri Mar 27, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Vladislav D. Kurilovich, William S. Cole, Roman M. Lutchyn, and Leonid I. Glazman

We develop a theory of the nonlocal conductance GRL(V) for a disordered Majorana wire tuned near the topological transition critical point. Under these conditions, the antisymmetric part of the differential conductance, [GRL(V)−GRL(−V)]/2, is the dominant one for a sufficiently long wire. This refle…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 094526] Published Fri Mar 27, 2026

item.title: Pairing-induced momentum-space magnetism and its implication in optical anomalous Hall effect in chiral superconductors
item.description:
Author(s): Bin Geng, Yang Gao, and Qian Niu

The intrinsic mechanisms of the magneto-optical Kerr signal in chiral superconductors often involve multiorbital degrees of freedom. Here, by considering a generic single-orbital and spinful Hamiltonian, we generalize the Onsager relation to obtain the necessary conditions for the optical anomalous …

item.title: Error resilience of fracton codes and near saturation of code-capacity threshold in three dimensions
item.description:
Author(s): Giovanni Canossa, Lode Pollet, Miguel A. Martin-Delgado, Hao Song, and Ke Liu

Fracton codes have been intensively studied as novel topological states of matter, yet their fault-tolerant properties remain largely unexplored. Here, we investigate the optimal thresholds of self-dual fracton codes, in particular, the checkerboard code, against stochastic Pauli noise. By utilizing…

item.description:

To date, all previous implementations relied on Abelian gauge fields; the role of non-Abelian gauge fields in tailoring spin-resolved topological transport remains relatively unexpl…

item.title: Majorana braiding simulations with projective measurements
item.description:
Author(s): Philipp Frey, Themba Hodge, Eric Mascot, and Stephan Rachel

Majorana modes are anyons that form on the boundary of topological superconductors; they can be utilized for quantum processing. However, the logical encoding scheme used to store information can restrict the set of allowed quantum gates, infringing on universality. Here, the authors explicate the theory required to map between the sparse and dense qubit encodings by using projective measurements. This allows for the implementation of a universal gate set, which is forbidden from either encoding individually. Through incorporation into a scalable simulation method, this provides the basis for numerical implementation of a topological quantum computer.


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 115153] Published Thu Mar 26, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Philipp Frey, Themba Hodge, Eric Mascot, and Stephan Rachel

Majorana modes are anyons that form on the boundary of topological superconductors; they can be utilized for quantum processing. However, the logical encoding scheme used to store information can restrict the set of allowed quantum gates, infringing on universality. Here, the authors explicate the theory required to map between the sparse and dense qubit encodings by using projective measurements. This allows for the implementation of a universal gate set, which is forbidden from either encoding individually. Through incorporation into a scalable simulation method, this provides the basis for numerical implementation of a topological quantum computer.


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 115153] Published Thu Mar 26, 2026

item.title: Resonant Edelstein and inverse Edelstein effects, charge-to-spin conversion, and spin pumping from chiral-spin modes
item.description:
Author(s): Mojdeh Saleh, Abhishek Kumar, Dmitrii L. Maslov, and Saurabh Maiti

The Rashba effect is the cornerstone of fascinating quantum phenomena like spin-charge conversion via the Edelstein effect and its inverse, the spin-Hall effect and its inverse, etc. These have proved to be the foundation for spintronics. However, much of these effects focused on the diffusive regime of electron motion. In spin-orbit coupled systems of the Rashba type, there is a special type of collective spin excitations, known as chiral-spin modes (CSMs). Here, the authors demonstrate that if such systems are probed close to the frequency of the CSMs, the spin-charge conversion effects exhibit a resonant enhancement. Using the polarization properties of the CSMs, the authors also suggest an architecture for spin pumping using THz pulses without the use of magnetic materials that offers control over the polarization of the injected spins. This work offers an avenue to push spintronics to the THz regime.


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 099602] Published Wed Mar 25, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Mojdeh Saleh, Abhishek Kumar, Dmitrii L. Maslov, and Saurabh Maiti

The Rashba effect is the cornerstone of fascinating quantum phenomena like spin-charge conversion via the Edelstein effect and its inverse, the spin-Hall effect and its inverse, etc. These have proved to be the foundation for spintronics. However, much of these effects focused on the diffusive regime of electron motion. In spin-orbit coupled systems of the Rashba type, there is a special type of collective spin excitations, known as chiral-spin modes (CSMs). Here, the authors demonstrate that if such systems are probed close to the frequency of the CSMs, the spin-charge conversion effects exhibit a resonant enhancement. Using the polarization properties of the CSMs, the authors also suggest an architecture for spin pumping using THz pulses without the use of magnetic materials that offers control over the polarization of the injected spins. This work offers an avenue to push spintronics to the THz regime.


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 099602] Published Wed Mar 25, 2026

item.title: Dispersion of collective modes in spinful fractional quantum Hall states on the sphere
item.description:
Author(s): Rakesh K. Dora and Ajit C. Balram

Fractional quantum Hall (FQH) fluids are prototypical examples of topologically ordered systems. The addition of a flavor degree of freedom, such as spin, adds to the richness of the FQH phenomenology. Here, the authors compute the dispersion of various collective excitations in the most prominent spinless and spinful FQH states. Intriguingly, they find that certain spinful FQH states host a novel collective mode, which they explain via a parton construction. The predicted parton mode can be observed in light scattering experiments.


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 115420] Published Mon Mar 23, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Rakesh K. Dora and Ajit C. Balram

Fractional quantum Hall (FQH) fluids are prototypical examples of topologically ordered systems. The addition of a flavor degree of freedom, such as spin, adds to the richness of the FQH phenomenology. Here, the authors compute the dispersion of various collective excitations in the most prominent spinless and spinful FQH states. Intriguingly, they find that certain spinful FQH states host a novel collective mode, which they explain via a parton construction. The predicted parton mode can be observed in light scattering experiments.


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 115420] Published Mon Mar 23, 2026
